Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at the event log examples  Case 1 to 5  Each case starts with OR followed by MS and QS Then AS occurs followed by IQC The IQC seems to loop back to AS multiple times

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For example in case 3 IQC is executed three times each followed by AS So this is a loop between AS and IQC

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After the IQC AS loop the process moves to TST If TST is followed by RT it loops back to TST In cases 1 3 and 5 RT occurs after TST leading back to TST So this is another loop between TST and RT

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After testing is successful there is PK Then LB is optional some cases like case 2 skip LB So there is a choice here either do LB or skip it

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Finally DP and D occur sequentially

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Start to MS and QS  OR is followed by MS and then QS Since OR is first then MS and QS are in sequence

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The loop would be a loop between AS and IQC The loop operator in POWL is A B which means execute A then either exit or execute B and then A again So here A is AS and B is IQC So the loop would be AS IQC

Name: Validation - PE
Text: That makes sense because each time after AS you can do IQC and loop back or exit

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The TST and RT loop is similar So the loop would be TST RT So TST is executed then either exit to PK or do RT and then TST again

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: After PK there is a choice between LB and skipping it So this is an exclusive choice between LB and a silent transition So X LB skip

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The main control flow is a sequence with loops and a choice

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The StrictPartialOrder nodes would be OR MS QS loop1 loop2 PK choice DP D

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The order between these nodes in the StrictPartialOrder would be OR MS QS loop1 loop2 PK choice DP D

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The process seems to be a linear sequence with loops and choices So all the nodes are in a strict order

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: loop1 is OperatorPOWL LOOP children AS IQC loop2 is OperatorPOWL LOOP children TST RT choice is OperatorPOWL XOR children LB skip

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The order edges would be OR MS MS QS QS loop1 loop1 loop2 loop2 PK PK choice choice DP DP D

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Testing against the event logs Take case 1 In the model OR is first Then MS then QS Then loop1 Then loop2 Then PK Then choice LB is executed Then DP D Which matches the log

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In case 2 the TST loop is not entered no RT so loop2 is executed once TST then PK The choice skips LB using the silent transition then DP D

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In case 4 No IQC or RT steps So the loop1 AS IQC is executed once AS then exit loop2 TST is executed once TST then exit then PK LB DP D

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: All cases are covered by the model
